sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: 5am.earth Trust Layer Targeting Vision 2030 KPIs

Deliverables: 5am.earth Foundation requests 10,000,000 ADA (hard cap) to build an open, Cardano-anchored trust layer for global agricultural supply chains., The trust layer combines Veridian self-sovereign identity for farmers and Agri-Entrepreneurs (AEs) with a Cardano on-chain satellite oracle delivering verified farm and crop data., The 18-month programme (M1 Stand-Up at Month 6, M2 Closed Loop at Month 12, M3 Self-Sustaining at Month 18) delivers across India, Cambodia, and Kenya, reaching 500,000 registered farmers and operating three named application paths during the funded period:..., The Foundation is the neutral steward of the open trust layer; commercial partners build on top of it.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): 5am.earth Foundation requests 10,000,000 ADA (hard cap) to build an open, Cardano-anchored trust layer for global agricultural supply chains.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): The trust layer combines Veridian self-sovereign identity for farmers and Agri-Entrepreneurs (AEs) with a Cardano on-chain satellite oracle delivering verified farm and crop data.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The 18-month programme (M1 Stand-Up at Month 6, M2 Closed Loop at Month 12, M3 Self-Sustaining at Month 18) delivers across India, Cambodia, and Kenya, reaching 500,000 registered farmers and operating three named application paths during the funded period:...
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): The Foundation is the neutral steward of the open trust layer; commercial partners build on top of it.

Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Globally, around 500 million smallholder farmers grow much of the world's food, yet remain largely outside the formal trust infrastructure that lenders, food companies, insurers, certifiers, and government programmes rely on.
Claim (technical, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Agricultural supply chains already depend on trust, but the trust infrastructure is fragmented.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "5am.earth Foundation requests 10,000,000 ADA (hard cap) to build an open, Cardano-anchored trust layer for global agricultural supply chains." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: an unresolved blocker (missing budget analysis) means costs or weak controls may outweigh the claimed benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
